content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the left ahead with the car positioned slightly right of center but well within track boundaries, continuing along the left-curving section.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 60, target = 60 + 5 = 65
- Waypoint 65: (3.4036, 4.2174)
- Car position: (4.0555, 3.5685)
- Bearing = atan2(4.2174 - 3.5685, 3.4036 - 4.0555) = atan2(0.6489, -0.6519)
- = 180 - atan2(0.6489, 0.6519) = 180 - 44.87° = 135.13°
- Current heading: 141.86°
- Delta = 135.13 - 141.86 = -6.73° → slight right correction needed

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves left ahead but waypoint 65 is slightly right of current heading (delta=-6.73°), so a small -5° steering correction maintains the waypoint line without oversteering.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track has a left-curving section around waypoints 55-70, heading southwest",
    "Car is consistently slightly right of center (~0.09-0.12m) but within safe margin (track width 0.76m)",
    "Full circuit ~17.7m, speed 1.5 is max and appropriate for moderate curves",
    "Use steering_angle=0 for |delta| <5° to avoid oscillation on near-straight sections",
    "Delta near -5 to -9° means gentle right correction — use steering_angle=-5",
    "Delta near +5 to +9° means gentle left correction — use steering_angle=+5",
    "Car is maintaining 1.5 speed well through this section",
    "Waypoints 60-70 show continuing left curve (south-southwest to west)",
    "5° steering is appropriate for delta 5-10° range to avoid overshoot",
    "When delta drops below 5°, immediately switch to 0 steering to prevent oscillation"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [4.296, 4.207, 4.133, 4.055],
    "y": [3.3848, 3.4532, 3.5094, 3.5685],
    "yaw": [141.148, 141.464, 141.698, 141.858]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": -6.73,
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": -5.0
}
```
